A trapper tells you the Missouri bends northwest for a thousand miles — a great highway.
A Missouri warrior gestures west — 'River long, very long. Goes to mountains.'
Otoe elders say the Platte River leads to Pawnee, then Spanish soldiers. Turn north.
Pawnee hunters warn of Spanish cavalry patrolling the Arkansas headwaters.
A lone fur trader coming east says the Mandan know the way to the Pacific.
The Mandan chief draws a map in the dirt showing a great river beyond the mountains.
An old Hidatsa warrior describes the Shining Mountains and a pass to the west.
A British trader warns that the northern route leads only to frozen tundra.
